title: Chunker Output Records
description: Output contract of the chunking tool, consumed by the embedding stage. The output is JSONL: one 'chunk' record per chunk, plus exactly one 'manifest' record per run written as the LAST line (it aggregates run statistics). Consumers must dispatch on 'record_type'. All character offsets are Python string indices (Unicode code points) into the 'text' field of the source PageRecord from the extraction schema.

SourceSpan.description: A contiguous character range on one source page that contributed to this chunk. A single-page chunk has exactly one span; a cross-page or section chunk has one span per contributing page, in reading order. Note: when overlap > 0, spans of adjacent chunks intentionally overlap.

page_index.description: 0-based physical page index. Foreign key to PageRecord.page_index.

page_label.description: Printed page label copied from PageRecord.page_label (e.g. 'iv', 'A-2'). Null if the PDF defines no labels. Prefer this (falling back to page_index+1) when rendering citations.

char_end.description: Exclusive end offset into PageRecord.text. Must be >= char_start.

SourceDoc.description: Citation-ready document metadata copied from the DocumentRecord, so the embedding/retrieval stage does not need to re-join against the document table.

title.description: DocumentRecord.metadata.Title. May be an empty string.

ChunkRecord.description: One embeddable unit. The 'text' field is the exact string to embed.

chunk_id.description: Stable ID: '{doc_id}::c{NNNN}' where NNNN is chunk_index zero-padded to at least 4 digits. Unique within a run; stable across reruns with identical input and config.

chunk_index.description: 0-based position of this chunk within the document, in reading order.

text.description: The chunk content. This exact string is the embedding input.

token_count.description: Number of tokens in 'text' measured with 'tokenizer_model', excluding special tokens (no CLS/SEP/BOS/EOS).

tokenizer_model.description: HF Hub repo id or local path of the tokenizer used for budgeting and counting, e.g. 'BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5'.

spans.description: Provenance: where in the source pages this chunk's text came from, in reading order. Single-element for within-page chunks.

section_path.description: Ordered TOC ancestry of the chunk's starting position, outermost first, e.g. ['Chapter 3', '3.2 Methods']. Empty array if the document has no TOC or the chunk precedes the first TOC entry.

SkippedPage.description: A page that produced no chunks, with the reason, so a side process (e.g. OCR) can fill the gap and the run can be repeated.

reason.description: needs_ocr: page flagged needs_ocr=true and --on-ocr-needed=skip. empty_text: page text was empty/whitespace-only. invalid_record: input line failed schema validation (non-strict mode).

ManifestRecord.description: Run provenance. Exactly one per output file, written as the LAST line (it aggregates totals). Lets stage 3 and future-you reproduce or audit the run.

created_at.description: UTC timestamp of the run, RFC 3339.

config.description: The effective configuration of the run (after defaults were applied).
